definition: The lowest structural element that spreads loads into the ground.
whyThisWord: A building stands on footings like a person stands on feet.
origin: From foot + -ing.
memoryTrick: A building needs feet: its feet are the footings.
example: The contractor must check the footing before the next stage of work proceeds.
exampleZh: 承包商必须在下一阶段施工开始前检查“基础；承台”。
australianUsage: Commonly used in Australian building and construction documents, drawings, specifications, site meetings, or inspections.

definition: The complete system that supports a building and transfers loads to the ground.
whyThisWord: It is the part on which the rest is founded.
origin: From Latin fundare, to establish or lay a base.
memoryTrick: Foundation = the whole supporting base.
example: The contractor must check the foundation before the next stage of work proceeds.

definition: A broad, flat structural element, usually made of reinforced concrete.

definition: A bevelled edge made by cutting away a sharp corner.

definition: Separation of bonded layers within a material or finish.
